ead.archdesc:
======= RMM07306Richard L. Schlegel papers7306Compiled by Phil McCrayDivision of Rare and Manuscript Collections
June 20002B Carl A. Kroch LibraryCornell UniversityIthaca, NY 14853rareref@cornell.eduURL:
humsxThis finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 19:01:32 UTC.ENG12/07/2018
This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)2007-05-29
converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Richard L. Schlegel papers
Schlegel, Richard LaMar
7306
11.9 cubic feet. 11.9 cubic feet.
[ca. 1960-1980] Correspondence, clippings, drafts of articles, legal briefs, business papers and records, subject files, photographs, film. Legal files pertaining to SCHLEGEL VS U.S., 1961-1969; files relating to PACE! A DIGEST OF SEX AND HUMOR, 1970; subject files on sexual topics in general and homosexual topics in particular; and erotic photographs and other materials. Collection material in English 2086428
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Phil McCray
Date completed:
May 1991
EAD encoding:
Mireille Lee, June 2000Mark Collins, Winter 2011
Date modified:
Jude Corina, March 2016
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Gay activist and editor and publisher of gay magazines. Born in Berrysburg, Pennsylvania, February 11, 1927 and died February 25, 2006.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Legal files, 1961-1969, pertaining to Schlegel vs U.S., a court case relating to Schlegel dismissal from U.S. Army employment and 1970 files relating to Pace! A Digest of Sex and Humor, for which Schlegel served as editor and co-publisher, including letters to the editor, business correspondence, and materials concerning the finances and advertising. Also, correspondence, erotic photographs and writing, and information on models collected for possible publication, some of which (when noted) came from John W. Emmerson (born April 23,1920, died April 18,1986) from Battle Creek, Michigan. Also clippings, a subject file, magazines, and books on sexual topics in general and homosexual topics in particular.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Correspondence and Personal Files
Box 1, folders 1-14; Box 6, folders 1-52; Box 7, folders 1-24; Box 8, folders 14-15, 22-25
Series II. Legal files
Box 2, folders 1-60; Box 3; Box 7, folder 33; Box 8, folders 16-18
Series III. Subject files
Box 3, folders 1-40; Box 4, folders 1-40; Box 7, folders 25-35; Box 8, folders 1-13, 19-21, 26-34; Box 12, folders 1-2, 4; Box 14
Series IV. Pace! files
Box 4, folders 41-52; Box 5, folders 1-24; Box 12, folders 3, 15-18
Series V. Erotica
Box 9, folders 1-45; Box 10, folders 1-69; Box 11, folders 1-40; Box 12, folders 5-14, 19-33; Box 13
SEPARATED MATERIAL
Cataloged in Human Sexuality Collection except items marked with an asterisk.
A Brief History of GAY: Canada's First Gay Tabloid by Donald McLeod
Day Is Ending: A Doctor's Love Shattered by Alzheimer's Disease, signed by author Walt Meyer*
Going for the Green: Selling in the 21st Century, signed by author Walt Meyer*
Trailblazing: America's First Openly Gay High School Coach, signed by author Eric Anderson
Cite As:
Richard L. Schlegel papers, #7306. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Access Restrictions:
Access to Boxes 9-10 restricted until 2035.
Access Restrictions:
Access to Box 11 restricted until 2030.
Restrictions on Use:
Materials in Boxes 9-10 may not be reproduced or put online.
Sex oriented businesses. Homosexuality and literature. Gays -- Employment. Gays -- Legal status, laws, etc. Sex (Psychology) Gay men. Homosexuality. United States. Army -- : Gays.
